seoTitle: How to Export Webflow Code and Host It Elsewhere
metaDescription: Learn how to export clean HTML, CSS, and JavaScript from Webflow and deploy it to a free static host like Netlify or Vercel.
intro: If your project only needs static hosting, exporting your Webflow code lets you skip ongoing CMS hosting fees while keeping your responsive design intact. This guide walks through generating the export package, uploading it to a static host, and fixing common issues like broken forms and asset paths. It's aimed at developers and agencies who want full ownership of their front-end files.

steps.heading: Trigger the code export
steps.body: Open the Webflow Designer, find the Export Code option in the top toolbar, and generate your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript package.

steps.heading: Download and inspect the zip
steps.body: Download the static site package and review the folder structure to confirm your templates, images, and scripts are all included.

steps.heading: Choose a free static host
steps.body: Sign up for a service like Netlify, Vercel, or GitHub Pages that supports drag-and-drop or Git-based static deployments.

steps.heading: Upload your files and connect a domain
steps.body: Drag your exported folder into the hosting dashboard, then map your custom domain to the new deployment.

steps.heading: Fix forms and broken assets
steps.body: Since Webflow's native form handling won't work outside its platform, set up an external form endpoint and check for any broken image paths before going live.

faq.question: Will my Webflow forms still work after export?
faq.answer: No, native Webflow forms rely on their backend, so you'll need to point form submissions to an external service like Netlify Forms or Formspree.

faq.question: Is exporting code available on every Webflow plan?
faq.answer: Code export typically requires a paid site plan, so check your current plan's capabilities before relying on this workflow.

faq.question: Will my responsive layout still work after export?
faq.answer: Yes, the exported CSS preserves your breakpoints, so your responsive design should render the same outside Webflow.

faq.question: Can I still edit the site visually after exporting?
faq.answer: No, once exported the code is static and any future changes need to be made either back in Webflow and re-exported, or directly in the code.
